content.content:
1	---
2	name: slack-ingest
3	description: "Pull recent posts from the Slack #trading-scheduled-updates channel and write them to a local digest file for downstream consumption by market-brief, daily-trade-rec, and positions-monitor. Use for 'ingest slack', 'pull slack updates', 'sync slack'. Also auto-called as Step 0 of /market-brief, /daily-trade-rec, and /positions-monitor."
4	allowed-tools: Read Write Bash(python3 *) mcp__3707e784-0d34-4a07-887c-f348b3366436__slack_search_channels mcp__3707e784-0d34-4a07-887c-f348b3366436__slack_read_channel
5	metadata:
6	  model: sonnet
7	---
8	# Slack Ingest
9	One-way pull: Slack scheduled-agent posts → local `{YYYY-MM-DD}/slack-digest-{date}.md`. The cloud agent fires 4x/day while the local machine may be off — this skill is what brings those intraday observations (regime pulses, position alerts, thesis breaches) into the local pipeline.
10	Local timezone UTC+8. Canonical channel name: `trading-scheduled-updates`.
11	---
12	## Step 1 — Resolve channel ID
13	```
14	slack_search_channels(query="trading-scheduled-updates")
15	```
16	Pick the exact-name match. If no match: write a digest file with body `CHANNEL NOT FOUND — configure #trading-scheduled-updates and re-run.` and stop.
17	## Step 2 — Pull last 24h of messages
18	```
19	slack_read_channel(channel_id=<id>, limit=100, response_format="concise")
20	```
21	Filter client-side:
22	- Keep messages with timestamps within the last 24h (UTC+8 day boundary is fine; cloud agent timestamps are UTC).
23	- Drop bot system messages (channel_join, etc.).
24	- Preserve original author, timestamp, and full text.
25	If >100 messages in the window, paginate via cursor.
26	## Step 3 — Categorize
27	Each message carries a leading tag from the cloud agent:
28	| Tag | Meaning | Priority |
29	|---|---|---|
30	| `[POSITION-ALERT]` | Stop buffer, thesis breach, vol-band-violation — requires local action | **HIGHEST** |
31	| `[ACTION-ITEMS]` | One concrete action per open position (synthesized by cloud agent) | **HIGHEST** |
32	| `[REGIME]` | Intraday regime pulse — VIX/MOVE/DXY/US10Y/CDX-HY/SPY readings | HIGH |
33	| `[THESIS]` | Thesis-variable delta for a named open position | HIGH |
34	| `[POTENTIAL-TRADES]` | Watchlist candidates + intraday setups flagged by cloud agent | MED |
35	| `[NEWS]` | News capture | MED |
36	| `[POSITION-STATE]` | Snapshots YOU pushed up — **ignore, do not ingest back** | — |
37	Untagged messages go under "Unclassified".
38	**For `[POSITION-ALERT]` messages:** extract and preserve:
39	- Ticker symbol (match against open positions)
40	- Alert sub-type if present: `vol-band-violation`, `thesis-breach`, `stop-buffer`, `catalyst-proximity`
41	- Timestamp (positions-monitor cites this for F12/F13 lineage)
42	**For `[REGIME]` messages:** extract and preserve the specific variable readings (VIX, MOVE, DXY, US10Y, CDX-HY, SPY values and direction) — market-brief Step 4 compares these against its own pull to detect staleness.
43	## Step 4 — Write digest
44	Path: `{YYYY-MM-DD}/slack-digest-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md` where `{YYYY-MM-DD}` is today in UTC+8. Create folder first: `mkdir -p {YYYY-MM-DD}`.
45	```markdown
46	# Slack Digest — {date} (ingested {ingest-time-utc+8})
47	Source channel: #trading-scheduled-updates
48	Window: last 24h ending {ingest-time}
49	Messages: {N} total ({alert-count} alerts, {regime-count} regime pulses)
50	## ⚠ Position Alerts [POSITION-ALERT]
51	[Surface FIRST if any exist — these gate new trade entries and trigger F12/F13 in positions-monitor.
52	 Format per alert: `{timestamp} | {ticker} | {sub-type} | {full message text}`
53	 If none: "No position alerts in window."]
54	## Action Items [ACTION-ITEMS]
55	[One action item per open position as synthesized by the cloud agent.
56	 Format: `{timestamp} | {ticker}: {action verb} — {numbers/rationale}`
57	 If none: "No action items in window."]
58	## Regime Pulse [REGIME]
59	[REGIME-tagged messages, newest first.
60	 Format: `{timestamp} | VIX={x} MOVE={x} DXY={x} US10Y={x} CDX-HY={x} SPY={x} | {direction/label}`
61	 Preserve numeric readings verbatim — market-brief Step 4 uses these for cross-check.]
62	## Thesis Variable Deltas [THESIS]
63	[THESIS-tagged messages, newest first.]
64	## News [NEWS]
65	[NEWS-tagged messages.]
66	## Potential Trades [POTENTIAL-TRADES]
67	[Watchlist candidates + intraday setups flagged by cloud agent.
68	 Format: `{timestamp} | {asset} {long|short}: trigger {x} | invalidation {y} | {why now}`
69	 If none: "No potential trades flagged in window."]
70	## Unclassified
71	[Messages without known tags.]
72	## Narrative Summary
73	[3–5 sentence synthesis written by Claude after reviewing all sections above. Cover:
74	 1. Regime status — what the most recent pulse says about market conditions and any flip since prior fire.
75	 2. Position health — whether any open positions are under pressure (alert or action-item driven); if all clear, say so.
76	 3. Opportunity read — whether any potential trade candidates are worth elevating to the next local session; note the strongest one if any.
77	 4. Any data gaps or anomalies (missing Grade A data, no POSITION-STATE in 72h, etc.).
78	 Keep it decision-focused. No padding. No repeating numbers already in the sections above — synthesize, don't restate.]
79	```
80	If zero messages in window: write a minimal file with body `No scheduled-update posts in last 24h window.` and exit.
81	## Step 5 — Report
82	One-line summary: `slack-digest written: N messages — {alert-count} POSITION-ALERTs, {action-count} ACTION-ITEMs, {regime-count} REGIME pulses, {news-count} NEWS, {trade-count} POTENTIAL-TRADEs`. Then output the Narrative Summary from the digest on the next line so the caller sees the key read at a glance.
83	---
84	## Downstream contract
85	Three skills consume `{today}/slack-digest-{today}.md`:
86	| Skill | What it reads | Treatment |
87	|---|---|---|
88	| `/market-brief` Step 4 | `[REGIME]` entries — compares numeric readings against its own Grade A pull | Digest = authoritative for last-observed value between runs; brief = authoritative for 20:00 canonical snapshot. Discrepancy >1σ → staleness flag. |
89	| `/daily-trade-rec` Step 5 item 7 | `[POSITION-ALERT]` entries — gates new entries in the same sleeve | Unresolved vol-band-violation or thesis-breach → blocks new sleeve entries until re-evaluated via trade-update. |
90	| `/positions-monitor` Step 2 | `[POSITION-ALERT]` entries — drives F12 (vol_band) and F13 (cloud_alert) flags | Timestamps cited in flag output for lineage back to cloud agent. |
91	All three treat a missing digest as non-fatal (Grade B optional context) — but invoke `/slack-ingest` proactively so the digest is present before their reads.

1	# BNMA Full Statistical Report
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-04-17
4	**Method:** Hierarchical Bayesian Network Meta-Analysis (4 parallel groups)
5	**Inference:** Full MCMC — 4 chains x 2,000 post-warmup draws = 8,000 posterior samples per group
6	**Variables:** 34 in main BNMA (22 registered + 12 candidates) | 6 in Stage F pipeline
7	**Studies:** 58 study rows across all tiers
8	
9	All R-hat < 1.01 and ESS > 400 for every parameter. Convergence confirmed.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 0. Statistical Validity Declarations
14	
15	### Valid Comparisons
16	
17	| Comparison | Method | Why Valid |
18	|------------|--------|-----------|
19	| Within-group pairwise P(μi > μj) | Joint posterior draws | Same model, same scale, correlated draws |
20	| Within-group rank distributions | Posterior rank computation per draw | Accounts for full uncertainty |
21	| ROPE practical significance | Posterior mass above/below threshold | Standard Bayesian decision rule |
22	| S vs T cross-group | Joint posterior draws | Both measure long-short portfolio Sharpe |
23	| Group heterogeneity (τ) comparison | Posterior τ draws | τ is unit-free relative to group scale |
24	| Posterior predictive checks | Bayesian p-values | Standard model adequacy test |
25	| I² heterogeneity per variable | Posterior I² draws | Standard meta-analytic heterogeneity |
26	
27	### NOT Statistically Possible
28	
29	| Claim | Reason |
30	|-------|--------|
31	| Ranking R variables against S/T variables | R measures risk-sizing improvement Sharpe; S/T measure long-short portfolio Sharpe. Different units. |
32	| Ranking Overlay variables against S/T/R | Overlay measures conditional Sharpe gain (a modifier). Different scale entirely. |
33	| Any "Overall Top N" mixing groups | Posterior means are on different measurement scales. P(top3) in a 3-variable group is trivially 1.0. |
34	| Overlay group Spearman grade-rank correlation | n=3 variables, all same grade. Degenerate — 1 degree of freedom. |
35	| Reliable pairwise discrimination in R group (ranks 2-8) | Posterior CIs overlap massively. Rank entropy is near-maximal. Only V027 vs field is distinguishable. |

## SLACK-INGEST SKILL

Location: `/c/Users/Lokis/OneDrive/Desktop/T.system/Trade/patches/slack-ingest-SKILL.md`

**Purpose:** One-way pull from Slack #trading-scheduled-updates channel → local `{YYYY-MM-DD}/slack-digest-{date}.md`

**Key Details:**
- Pulls messages from the last 24 hours (UTC+8 day boundary)
- Categorizes posts by tag: `[POSITION-ALERT]`, `[ACTION-ITEMS]`, `[REGIME]`, `[THESIS]`, `[POTENTIAL-TRADES]`, `[NEWS]`, `[POSITION-STATE]` (ignored)
- Extracts critical data for downstream consumption:
  - `[POSITION-ALERT]` → feeds `positions-monitor` (F12/F13 flags)
  - `[REGIME]` → feeds `market-brief` (staleness cross-check)
  - `[ACTION-ITEMS]` → extracted for position management
- Outputs markdown digest with:
  - Position alerts (HIGHEST priority)
  - Action items (HIGHEST priority)
  - Regime pulse with numeric readings (VIX, MOVE, DXY, US10Y, CDX-HY, SPY)
  - Thesis variable deltas
  - News and potential trades
  - 3–5 sentence narrative summary (regime status, position health, opportunity read, data gaps)
- Fires 4×/day: midnight, 8am, 12pm, 4pm UTC+8

**Downstream Contract:**
- `/market-brief` — reads `[REGIME]` entries, compares numeric readings for staleness detection
- `/daily-trade-rec` — reads `[POSITION-ALERT]` entries to gate new entries in same sleeve
- `/positions-monitor` — reads `[POSITION-ALERT]` entries to drive vol-band and cloud-alert flags

  - **Feed to 2026-10-14 audit:**
